key_insight: Molecular dynamics shows that nanofluid nanoparticle shape stability depends strongly on particle size and temperature, with 28-atom particles becoming unstable while 42- and 56-atom particles remain shell-stable.
engineering_relevance: These results help guide the selection of nanoparticle size and operating temperature for designing thermally robust polymeric nanofluids with stable structure.
method: Ran molecular dynamics simulations of polymeric nanofluids with nonrigid nanoparticles in a polymer melt.
Computed one-dimensional radial distribution functions (RDF) and three-dimensional angle-dependent RDFs (ARDF).
Compared nanoparticle behavior with and without the base fluid across temperatures from T = 1.2 to 1.8.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports temperature-dependent structural changes: the 28-atom nanoparticle loses its concentric shell structure in the polymer base fluid, while the 42- and 56-atom nanoparticles preserve it.
watch_item: The findings are based on simulated nanoscale systems and may not transfer directly to real fluids without experimental validation and sensitivity checks on interaction parameters.
paper_title: The Radial Distribution Functions of Nanofluids: Molecular Dynamics Simulations
